10 Useful extension methods in C#

Technical

Jan 19, 2023

We use extension methods to extend the functionalities of existing classes in a clean and reusable way, and also to extend functionalities of closed classes, classes defined in other libraries, which we cannot modify.

In this article, we will take a look at 10 useful extension methods that can help you write more efficient and maintainable code in C#.

I will write each extension method as a separate article and will go deep dive into them, this article will be the main article linking to them.

Method 1: DistinctBy
medium.com
Method 2: ToDataTable
medium.com
Method 3: OrderByField
medium.com
Method 4: ToJson
medium.com
Method 5: FromJson
This simple extension method allows you to easily convert a JSON string to an object in C#medium.com
Useful Extension Method 6: Batch
This extension method allows for efficient processing and handling of large collections of data by breaking them into…medium.com
Method 7: TimeoutAfter
TimeoutAfter extension method is a useful utility method that allows you to set a timeout for a specific Taskmedium.com
Method 8: Move
This “Move” extension method is a useful tool for manipulating the order of items within a list in C#medium.com
Method 9: IsNumeric
This is a useful extension method in C# that can be used to check if a string contains only numeric charactersmedium.com
Method 10: RemoveSpecialCharacters
This extension method can be a useful tool for cleaning up strings in C#. It allows for easy removal of special…medium.com

In conclusion, extension methods can be a powerful tool for developers, allowing them to add new functionality to existing classes and types. The methods listed in this article are just a few examples of the many extension methods that can be created in C#, but they can be useful in a variety of situations. By using these extension methods, developers can make their code more concise, readable, and maintainable.

Thanks for reading!

Sources:

Extension Methods - C# Programming Guide
Extension methods enable you to "add" methods to existing types without creating a new derived type, recompiling, or…learn.microsoft.com
